explicit_text: I was drafted into the United States Army at age twenty during the Korean War era. I went through basic training at Camp Roberts, California, and later served as a cadre man training new recruits. My father was also an Army officer and a lifer, which gave me some perspective, but I never wanted to make the Army a career. After the war ended, I was assigned to a security guard unit until the camp closed, and I was eventually discharged as a PFC.
